figures.caption: Table 8: Hyperparameter sensitivity analysis on HCPilot dataset.

Figure 1: The five-layer EGPF architecture. Behavioral signals are ingested (Layer 1), composed via category-theoretic functors (Layer 2), processed through the multi-agent game-theoretic engine (Layer 3), used to condition generative AI personalization (Layer 4), and monitored via information-theoretic feedback (Layer 5). The dashed arrow represents the closed-loop recalibration cycle.
Table 1: Computational complexity per interaction step.

Figure 2: Top: Nature draws physician type θ with prior probabilities. Bottom: Payoff matrix (uP , uD) for each action–type pair. Green boxes indicate type-optimal actions (diagonal dominance confirms the value of personalization).

Figure 3: Replicator dynamics showing population shift after competitor biologic entry at t = 100. Formulary-sensitive physicians (θ3) become dominant as cost competition intensifies, triggering EGPF recalibration.
Figure 5: Engagement prediction AUC-ROC across datasets. EGPF-Full achieves 34% relative improvement over static segmentation and 13% over the contextual bandit baseline.
Figure 6: Cross-therapeutic transfer performance. The natural transformation η enables strong generalization especially in low-data regimes.
